behavior_summary: U8P_MACE2::look is an inspect/U8P_MACE2::look handler that, when the player examines the object, emits a single short bark/text identifying the item as "mace " and then the process suspends/ends.
There is no conditional logic, no menu interaction, and no calls to other scripts or engine routines from this process.

Item metadata associated with this object (not used by the U8P_MACE2::look process itself) indicates this entry represents a mace-type weapon (example properties: baseDamage 4, damageModifier 11, damageType blunt, treasureChance 9).
notable_control_flow: Single-entry straight-line flow: trigger -> bark "mace " -> suspend/terminate.
Process runs under the engine's U8P_MACE2::look/inspect execution form (processType 0x0208).
open_questions: Is the trailing space in the bark text "mace " intentional or an artifact of extraction?
Are the listed item properties (damage, modifier, type, treasure chance) consumed elsewhere in gameplay systems even though this script does not reference them?
Was this object intended to have more complex inspect text/behavior that was removed or simplified?

main_function.name: U8P_MACE2::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the short in-world label when the player inspects/examines the mace object; display a single bark/text and then end/suspend the U8P_MACE2::look process.
entry_conditions: Triggered by the player's U8P_MACE2::look/inspect action on the object (engine U8P_MACE2::look process, processType 0x0208).
Inferred: activated when the player targets or examines the item in-game.

function_map.name: U8P_MACE2::look
function_map.role: Short-label/inspect handler that displays a single bark identifying the object as a mace.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: U8P_MACE2::look is an inspect/U8P_MACE2::look handler that, when the player examines the object, emits a single short bark/text identifying the item as "mace " and then the process suspends/ends.
